mappings.rationaleAI: The state's unitary versus federal structures address national political systems (A.1.b), while the range of state forms from prehistoric stateless to modern nation-states connects to types and models of political systems (A.2).

Monarchy as a system of hereditary sovereign rule addresses types of political systems (A.2), while constitutional monarchies embedded in parliamentary democracies connect to the executive branch (B.3).
The EU as a supranational political and economic union with Commission, Parliament, Council, and Court of Justice directly exemplifies supranational political systems (A.1.a).
Government's legislative, executive, and judicial branches directly address the branches of government (B), while its constitutional frameworks and diverse forms connect to political systems (A).

Democracy as a system of government with direct, representative, and constitutional forms addresses types of political systems (A.2), parliamentary rule (B.1), and the legislature as the people's representative body (B.2).

The UK's Westminster parliamentary model exemplifies the concentration of legislative and executive functions in parliamentary rule (B.1), while its constitutional monarchy and devolution illustrate national political systems (A.1.b).
The US federal republic exemplifies the federal state system (A.1.b) with its constitutional separation of powers across legislature (B.2), executive (B.3), and judiciary (B.4).
Cities with complex systems of governance, municipal finance, and administrative complexity directly address urban governments (A.1.c).

Rome's constitutional evolution from monarchy through republic to empire exemplifies types of political systems (A.2), its multi-continental empire addresses supranational systems (A.1.a), and its constitutional institutions connect to branches of government (B).
The Holy Roman Empire as a complex, elective, multi-ethnic polity with decentralized power exemplifies supranational political systems (A.1.a) and a distinctive type of political system (A.2).
The British Empire as the largest empire in history with its dominion system and colonial governance directly addresses supranational political systems including empires and commonwealths (A.1.a).

The Soviet Union as a transcontinental communist federal state addresses the federal state system (A.1.b) and represents a distinctive type of political system based on one-party communist rule (A.2).
China as a unitary communist state governed by the CCP exemplifies the unitary nation-state system (A.1.b) and a distinctive type of political system (A.2).
The Ottoman Empire as a transcontinental state with its millet system of confessional autonomy addresses supranational political systems (A.1.a) and a distinctive type of political system (A.2).
Law's coverage of legal institutions and the judiciary, including constitutional and administrative frameworks, directly connects to the judiciary as a branch of government (B.4).
Saudi Arabia as an absolute Islamic monarchy integrating dynastic rule with Wahhabi Islamic law represents a distinctive type of political system combining religious and monarchical authority (A.2).
Iran as a theocratic Islamic republic with elected and unelected institutions and ultimate authority vested in the supreme leader represents a distinctive type of political system (A.2).

India as a federal parliamentary republic with 22 scheduled languages addresses the federal state system (A.1.b) and parliamentary rule concentrating legislative and executive functions (B.1), with its parliament exemplifying the legislature (B.2).
Australia as a federal parliamentary constitutional monarchy addresses the federal state system (A.1.b) and parliamentary rule (B.1).
Germany as a federal republic with its distinctive parliamentary structure addresses the federal state system (A.1.b).
France as a unitary republic with centralized governance addresses the unitary nation-state system (A.1.b) and its semi-presidential model represents a distinctive type of political system (A.2).
Washington as the first US president who set foundational executive precedents including peaceful transfer of power and a two-term limit directly addresses the executive branch (B.3).

Augustus's establishment of the principate, preserving republican facades while concentrating power, addresses types of political systems (A.2) and the executive branch through institutional consolidation (B.3).
Nigeria as a federal republic of 36 states encompassing over 250 ethnic groups addresses the federal state system (A.1.b).
Brazil as a federal republic with a history spanning empire to military dictatorship to democratic consolidation addresses the federal state system (A.1.b).
Mexico as a federal republic with a history of one-party PRI rule and democratic transition addresses the federal state system (A.1.b) and types of political systems (A.2).
New York City as the most populous US city with five boroughs and complex governance directly addresses urban governments (A.1.c).
The nation as collective social organization defined by shared identity provides the conceptual foundation for national political systems (A.1.b).
